font_pairs.name: Fraunces + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Fraunces' variable optical sizing lets the H1 land as a true display face (at opsz 144 it has expressive contrast), while Inter handles UI and body with neutral precision. The pairing has the editorial gravitas of Tiempos+Soehne without the licensing cost. Use Fraunces only at 32px+ to avoid muddy small-size rendering.

font_pairs.rationale: Alfa Slab One is a single-weight heavy slab serif -- it has the impact of a vintage western poster. Inter body. Use for agency and marketing sites that want playful-brutalist display character; never use Alfa Slab One below 36px (the slab serifs blob) or for body (it has no body-weight).

font_pairs.name: Big Shoulders Stencil Display + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Big Shoulders Stencil Display is the stencilled variant of Big Shoulders -- it has military / industrial signage character. Inter body. Use for agency and portfolio sites that want brutalist display with stencil character; the stencilled forms demand tight tracking (-0.03em) and tight leading (1.05).

font_pairs.name: Bowlby One + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Bowlby One is a single-weight rounded display sans -- it has the heavy-round character of a 1950s circus poster. Inter body. Use for playful-brutalist agency and marketing sites that want display impact with friendly round forms; never use Bowlby One below 36px where the heavy weights blob.

font_pairs.name: Instrument Serif + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Instrument Serif is a single-weight display serif with calligraphic italics -- it's the free equivalent of a Tiempos Headline moment, with more personality than Source Serif and less theatricality than Playfair. Inter body. Use for SaaS marketing and agency sites that want editorial display without committing to Fraunces' variable complexity.

font_pairs.name: Instrument Serif + DM Sans
font_pairs.rationale: Instrument Serif display over DM Sans body is a warmer alternative to Instrument+Inter -- DM Sans's geometric body matches Instrument's calligraphic display better than Inter's clinical neutrality. Use for agency and ecommerce sites that want editorial display with warmth. Use the italic for emphasis; the regular alone is plain.

font_pairs.name: Recursive + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Recursive is a single superfamily with sans and mono axes (CASL, MONO, slnt, wght) -- it's the most variable font on Google Fonts. Inter body. Use for SaaS and docs that want a single-family system across display, body, and code; Recursive's casual axis (CASL) lets the display get more characterful at heavier weights.

font_pairs.name: Work Sans + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Work Sans was designed as a free Soehne alternative -- it's a quiet grotesque that works at body sizes and display. Inter body. Use for docs and SaaS marketing where you want a Soehne-adjacent feel without licensing; the pairing is more characterful than Inter+Inter without breaking the modern-SaaS aesthetic.

font_pairs.name: Bodoni Moda + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Bodoni Moda is a contemporary Bodoni revival with a variable opsz axis (6..96) -- it has the didone contrast of Playfair but with more weight range and a modern-magazine feel. Inter body. Use for fashion / luxury / editorial contexts where Playfair feels overused; tighten Bodoni Moda to -0.04em at large sizes.

font_pairs.name: Climate Crisis + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Climate Crisis is a variable display sans designed for protest signage -- it has a single weight axis (0-1000) and goes from hairline to ultra-heavy. Inter body. Use for nonprofit and activist agency sites that want brutalist display with ideological character; the face is intentionally political, so use it on briefs that match.

font_pairs.name: Bricolage Grotesque + DM Sans
font_pairs.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque display (variable opsz 12..96) over DM Sans body is a fully-geometric pairing -- Bricolage at opsz 96 has expressive character that DM Sans body complements without competing. Use for agency and portfolio sites where the H1 has to feel hand-considered; the variable opsz lets the same face scale to body if needed.

font_pairs.name: Rubik + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Rubik is a geometric sans designed for the Rubik's Cube brand -- it has subtly rounded corners that read as friendly without being childish. Inter body. Use for consumer SaaS and personal sites that want geometric character with warmth; tighten Rubik to -0.02em at large sizes for the engineered-display look.

font_pairs.name: Unbounded + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Unbounded is a variable display sans with letterforms inspired by Y2K / cyberpunk -- it has the rounded-square character of a 2000s tech logo. Inter body. Use for Web3 / AI / future-tech agency and marketing sites where the H1 needs to reinforce the brand's future-facing identity. Tighten to -0.02em at large sizes.

font_pairs.name: Syne + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Syne is a contemporary art-school grotesque with deliberately eccentric letterforms -- the lowercase 'a' is asymmetric, the 'g' is open. Inter body. Use for agency and portfolio sites that want display character without going full-display (Bebas / Anton); Syne's brutalist personality reads as 'designed by a person'.

font_pairs.name: Caveat + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Caveat is a handwriting face with full weight range (400-700) -- it reads as 'signed by a human' rather than 'typed'. Inter body. Use for personal portfolios and warm marketing sites where the H1 needs to feel hand-written; never use Caveat below 24px (the strokes blob) or for body text. Caveat's italic is the most expressive weight.

font_pairs.name: Patrick Hand + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Patrick Hand is a single-weight handwriting face with a slightly naïve, schoolbook quality -- it reads as friendly without being childish. Inter body. Use for personal sites, nonprofit, and education contexts where the H1 needs warmth; never use Patrick Hand below 28px (the strokes are too thin for small sizes).

font_pairs.name: Architects Daughter + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Architects Daughter is a single-weight handwriting face with a technical / architect's-sketch quality -- it reads as 'drawn by an engineer' rather than 'written by a calligrapher'. Inter body. Use for personal dev blogs and small-agency sites where the H1 needs sketchy character; never use below 28px.

font_pairs.name: Reem Kufi + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Reem Kufi is a contemporary Kufi face designed for Arabic and Latin -- the Latin has geometric Kufi-inspired letterforms that read as 'luxury-meets-engineered'. Inter body. Use for agency and luxury marketing sites that want display character with cultural depth; tighten to -0.02em at large sizes.

font_pairs.name: Cormorant + IBM Plex Mono
font_pairs.rationale: Cormorant display over IBM Plex Mono body is an unexpected but powerful pairing -- the calligraphic high-contrast serif over the technical monospace body creates a tension between editorial and engineering. Use for agency and portfolio sites that want to read as 'designed by a person who codes'; skip for general SaaS where the combo reads as costume.

font_pairs.name: Fraunces + JetBrains Mono
font_pairs.rationale: Fraunces display over JetBrains Mono body is an editorial-meets-dev pairing -- Fraunces at opsz 144 has expressive display character, JetBrains Mono body reads as 'code as content'. Use for agency and portfolio sites of design-engineers; the combo is costumey on general SaaS but powerful on personal sites where the brand is 'I design and I code'.

font_pairs.name: Major Mono Display + Inter
font_pairs.rationale: Major Mono Display is a single-weight monospace display face with deliberately crude letterforms -- it reads as 'late-90s terminal poster'. Inter body. Use for agency and portfolio sites that want brutalist display with retro-tech character; never use below 36px (the crude forms need size to land). Pair with Inter for the UI.

font_pairs.name: Instrument Serif + Source Serif 4
font_pairs.rationale: Instrument Serif display over Source Serif 4 body is a contemporary editorial serif+serif pairing -- Instrument's calligraphic display over Source Serif's mechanical body creates contrast without changing classification. Use for publication and agency sites that want to read as 'written, designed, and engineered by humans'; tighten Instrument to -0.02em.
